title: file: get and put call std::fs blocking I/O on async executor thread
description: Trigger: any tokio current_thread executor or busy multi-thread executor with many concurrent get calls. std::fs::read and write_secret_file are called synchronously inside async fn, blocking the executor thread for full disk I/O duration. TTL=0 means get can be called on every request. The async fn signature implies yielding; it does not. Fix: wrap both in tokio::task::spawn_blocking, matching what local-signing already does for RSA.
